rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in annex agree blocks; use component type scopes for assumptions/guarantees involving ports, implementation scopes for bindings.
Use assign only for output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope; avoid guarantee statements in implementation scope.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Do not encode AADL properties like timing or unit tokens as AGREE variables.
Avoid unsafe Boolean or numeric encodings of event ports or opaque data types.
Write type scope guarantees relating visible outputs to visible inputs; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Implementation-level bindings use assign statements; type-level contracts use assumptions and guarantees.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assumptions and equations.
Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Period, Priority) as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
